jokes.text: Oh, my god! I can't believe it! I just won an art contest. Aah!
jokes.setup: Character excitedly announces winning an art contest
jokes.punchline: Revealed to be a prank setup by Jim
jokes.explanation: Classic Jim prank setup - gets someone to say something embarrassing, solid execution

jokes.text: I got her! Ooohhh... not cool, dwight. Not cool, man.
jokes.setup: Jim's successful prank on a coworker
jokes.punchline: Dwight's moral disapproval of the prank
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Jim's glee contrasted with Dwight's unexpected moral stance

jokes.text: Tiptoeing around corporate, it is a ballet. When I am breaking all the rules, I'm break dancing.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining his approach to corporate rules
jokes.punchline: Elaborate dance metaphor that sounds profound but is actually silly
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Michael wordplay - sounds wise but reveals his childish mindset, very quotable

jokes.text: Guys, I'm scared. I'm really scared. I think I'm growing into a giant, because look at this normal-size coffee cup. Looks so tiny in my giant hand now.
jokes.setup: Michael expressing genuine fear about his size
jokes.punchline: He's holding an espresso cup, which are naturally tiny
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael obliviousness - doesn't realize he's holding an espresso cup

jokes.text: I do. - Okay, how about a woman? Pam?
jokes.setup: Michael asks who wants lunch, Andy volunteers
jokes.punchline: Michael immediately dismisses Andy for a woman
jokes.explanation: Michael's casual sexism and dismissiveness, awkward but predictable

jokes.text: At dunder mifflin, there is a very strict No-Lunch-With-The-Boss policy. And I don't know who instituted it. I think it started right after my predecessor stepped down.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining a workplace policy
jokes.punchline: He's completely oblivious that HE is the reason for the policy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael delusion - can't see that his behavior created the policy against him

jokes.text: I don't do lunch. I'm doing five small meals a day now. Now that I'm back to doing the job of a temp again, I find that food is one thing I can control.
jokes.setup: Ryan declining lunch
jokes.punchline: Reveals his sad need to control something after his fall from grace
jokes.explanation: Great character work - Ryan's pretentiousness now mixed with pathetic desperation

jokes.text: I am accounting on you to go to lunch with me.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to convince accounting department
jokes.punchline: Terrible pun on 'accounting/counting'
jokes.explanation: Weak pun, typical Michael dad joke territory but not particularly clever

jokes.text: We are office drones. We are office drones. All we do is work... is work... if you don't take out his battery, He just keeps going all day
jokes.setup: Michael ranting about being office drones
jokes.punchline: Jim treats him like a malfunctioning robot, removing his 'battery'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual gag - Jim literally treating Michael like a broken robot

jokes.text: Oh, no, your battery fell out. I was just learning to love.
jokes.setup: Michael's 'battery' falls out
jokes.punchline: His dramatic robot death scene about learning to love
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael melodrama - treating a silly prank with movie-death seriousness

jokes.text: Erin, how many times do I have to tell you? It is not necessary for you to ask how we are doing every time you interact with us.
jokes.setup: Erin's overly friendly greeting
jokes.punchline: Oscar's frustrated correction of her politeness
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - Erin's earnestness vs Oscar's condescension

jokes.text: You're right. I'm sorry. Now, how can I help you?
jokes.setup: Erin apologizing for asking how people are
jokes.punchline: Immediately asks essentially the same question
jokes.explanation: Great Erin character moment - completely misses the point of Oscar's criticism

jokes.text: I will take your silence to mean that you're all hiding something.
jokes.setup: Dwight asking about a map to Ohio
jokes.punchline: Assumes silence means guilt rather than indifference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight paranoia - turning normal silence into conspiracy

jokes.text: There's only a handful of reasons why someone would ever go to a courthouse in ohio, and not be charged with a crime. To claim an inheritance from a deceased relative. To obtain a learner's permit at age 14 1/2 instead of 15.
jokes.setup: Dwight listing reasons to go to an Ohio courthouse
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and incorrect reasons, including the learner's permit detail
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight false expertise with weirdly specific wrong information

jokes.text: Erin, let me see your birth certificate.
jokes.setup: Dwight's theory about learner's permits
jokes.punchline: Immediately demands Erin's birth certificate to verify age
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - Dwight takes his theory to immediate absurd action

jokes.text: You know what I want to do today? I want to marry you. I had just woken up. I didn't look cute.
jokes.setup: Kelly describing Ryan's romantic proposal
jokes.punchline: Her immediate concern about her appearance rather than the romance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kelly moment - more worried about looking cute than the proposal itself

jokes.text: That's how I knew he meant it.
jokes.setup: Kelly explaining why Ryan's proposal was genuine
jokes.punchline: Her logic that looking bad proved his sincerity
jokes.explanation: Clever Kelly logic - twisted but somehow makes sense for her character

jokes.text: No work! No work! No work! I come in here to relieve frustration. Ooh, ahh, ahh, ooh!
jokes.setup: Michael being asked about work in his cafe disco
jokes.punchline: His dramatic rejection with suggestive grunting
jokes.explanation: Typical Michael inappropriate behavior, but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Lock's broken, so...
jokes.setup: Michael inviting people to his cafe disco
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions the door doesn't actually lock
jokes.explanation: Good understated detail about the shabby nature of his 'disco'

jokes.text: Gotta keep yourself dehydrated - That's rule number one.
jokes.setup: Michael offering espresso
jokes.punchline: Completely backwards advice about dehydration
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael false wisdom - sounds confident but is completely wrong

jokes.text: When I was in charge, this place was like Dave & Buster's. People just hanging out, having fun, eatin' apps. I don't know, it's like... Dave died or something.
jokes.setup: Michael comparing the office to Dave & Buster's
jokes.punchline: Creates elaborate metaphor where 'Dave died' explains the fun being gone
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Michael metaphor - turns corporate restaurant into personal tragedy

jokes.text: Daddy's here, and daddy's gonna take care of you. Please don't refer to yourself as our daddy. I am your big daddy...
jokes.setup: Michael calling himself daddy
jokes.punchline: Jim's reasonable objection makes Michael double down with 'big daddy'
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment but predictable Michael inappropriate escalation

jokes.text: Widdle Andy is afwaid. Andy's afwaid? Yes. Are you all afwaid?
jokes.setup: Michael babying his employees
jokes.punchline: Baby talk that makes everyone uncomfortable, Andy's confused response
jokes.explanation: Standard Michael inappropriate behavior, not particularly clever

jokes.text: A place where unattractive and attractive people can get together to meet, to greet, to see the ones that you love, to love the ones that you see.
jokes.setup: Michael describing his cafe disco
jokes.punchline: Unnecessarily mentions unattractive people and gets lost in his own rhyme scheme
jokes.explanation: Michael's attempt at poetry shows his good intentions but poor execution

jokes.text: Why don't you get over lunch, Oscar? Everybody else is past it.
jokes.setup: Oscar asking if this is punishment for not having lunch
jokes.punchline: Michael claims everyone else is 'past it' when he clearly isn't
jokes.explanation: Good irony - Michael obviously not over the lunch rejection himself

jokes.text: This is like a haunted coffeehouse? No, Dwight is confusing you. It's more of a disco. A haunted disco!
jokes.setup: Confusion about what the cafe disco actually is
jokes.punchline: Dwight makes it worse by adding 'haunted' to disco
jokes.explanation: Good escalating confusion with Dwight making everything weirder

jokes.text: It is a daytime disco on the ground floor of an industrial office building. It's a cafe disco. So, like, a disco cafe. No, no. Not even close.
jokes.setup: Michael's elaborate explanation of his concept
jokes.punchline: Jim's logical rephrasing is completely rejected by Michael
jokes.explanation: Great Michael logic - somehow 'disco cafe' is completely different from 'cafe disco'

jokes.text: This is a no-work zone. Please respect the lei.
jokes.setup: Michael stopping work talk in his space
jokes.punchline: References a lei he's apparently wearing as authority
jokes.explanation: Funny visual of Michael wearing a lei and treating it as official authority

jokes.text: Kevin, stay. Kevin, come. Kevin, stay. Kevin, come! Stay, stay. Come on, right now. Cookie, Kevin. Cookie.
jokes.setup: Angela and Michael both trying to get Kevin to come/stay
jokes.punchline: They literally treat Kevin like a dog, with Michael offering a 'cookie'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dehumanizing of Kevin - both characters treating him like a pet

jokes.text: I wanted a cookie.
jokes.setup: Kevin being fought over like a dog
jokes.punchline: His simple, genuine disappointment about the non-existent cookie
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin moment - completely misses the degrading treatment, just wants cookie

jokes.text: Now I know what the founders of Philip Morris felt like. You just want to give people a smooth, fun way to relax. And suddenly you're just some terrible monster.
jokes.setup: Michael feeling persecuted about his cafe disco
jokes.punchline: Compares himself to tobacco company executives
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comparison - Michael equating dance floor to cigarettes, completely tone-deaf

jokes.text: Tell him I'm going dancing downstairs in the storage closet between the bathrooms that used to be a utility shower, and he should join me there.
jokes.setup: Michael inviting Bob Vance to his disco
jokes.punchline: The increasingly pathetic description of the actual location
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Michael's disco dreams with harsh reality of the location

jokes.text: Oh, *** god! Oh, no, no, no. Oh, wow! What did you do?
jokes.setup: Dancing in the cramped disco space
jokes.punchline: Phyllis gets injured, Michael's panicked reaction
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy setup but fairly predictable outcome of dancing in small space

jokes.text: Who's Philip? No, no, no. Who tipped you over? Was it Philip?
jokes.setup: Andy arriving to help injured Phyllis
jokes.punchline: He mishears 'Phyllis' as 'Philip' and creates imaginary culprit
jokes.explanation: Good Andy confusion moment - mishearing creates entirely wrong scenario

jokes.text: Back injuries are common. Not as common as knee injuries, but more common than wrist injuries. I don't need a history lesson, okay? What do you think history is?
jokes.setup: Dwight offering injury statistics during crisis
jokes.punchline: Michael's frustrated response leads to Dwight's philosophical question about history
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight - inappropriately pedantic during emergency, then gets philosophical

jokes.text: But most importantly, we need to get her some medical attention ASAP. Stat. Rrroar! Ramming speed.
jokes.setup: Michael taking charge of medical emergency
jokes.punchline: Adds unnecessary military/naval terminology and sound effects
jokes.explanation: Michael turning medical emergency into action movie, typical overcompensation

jokes.text: If you want to get sick, you go to a hospital.
jokes.setup: Discussion about taking Phyllis to hospital
jokes.punchline: Dwight's backwards logic about hospitals making people sick
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight pseudo-wisdom that sounds logical but is completely wrong

jokes.text: Okay, fine. Phyllis did injure herself. But she injured herself having fun. And I don't think she would trade that memory for anything.
jokes.setup: Michael defending his disco after injury
jokes.punchline: Claims Phyllis would cherish the memory of getting hurt
jokes.explanation: Michael's delusional spin on injury shows his inability to accept responsibility

jokes.text: You all took a life here today. You did. The life of the party.
jokes.setup: Michael's dramatic response to disco shutdown
jokes.punchline: Reveals he means 'life of the party' not actual death
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection pun - makes you think someone died, then reveals wordplay

jokes.text: Cafe disco. More like crapped disco.
jokes.setup: Creed commenting on the failed disco
jokes.punchline: Weak rhyming insult
jokes.explanation: Weak pun that feels forced and unfunny, even for Creed's standards

jokes.text: There's girls in there. Where? The other room. Down the hall. There's girls in there? What'd I just say? You get me my sandwich? Forget the sandwich. Girls. Girls!
jokes.setup: Kevin discovers women at the disco
jokes.punchline: Andy's immediate priority shift from sandwich to girls
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Andy's shallow priorities, but fairly predictable

jokes.text: I guess they got what they want. I am eating alone. Might as well be dinner.
jokes.setup: Michael eating alone while everyone goes to disco
jokes.punchline: His pathetic attempt to seem unbothered
jokes.explanation: Standard Michael self-pity, not particularly innovative or funny

jokes.text: This shirt wasn't doing you any favors.
jokes.setup: Dwight treating injured Phyllis
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate comment about her appearance while giving medical care
jokes.explanation: Dwight's inappropriate honesty during medical treatment, mildly funny

jokes.text: Like children singing Christmas carols.
jokes.setup: Oscar describing Phyllis's teeth grinding
jokes.punchline: Unexpectedly poetic comparison that's oddly specific
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly beautiful and specific comparison from Oscar, well-crafted

jokes.text: My grandfather was told that Diamond Dancer would never race again. They were wrong. He came in ninth in the Apple Creek Derby. And his jerky came in third the following year. A majestic beast. So fast, so tender.
jokes.setup: Dwight telling inspirational horse story
jokes.punchline: Horse came in ninth (not great) and was made into jerky, mixing triumph with tragedy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dwight story - starts inspirational, becomes absurd, ends touching but dark

jokes.text: I just don't like the general spirit of music.
jokes.setup: Angela explaining why she won't dance
jokes.punchline: Rejects the entire concept of music itself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Angela line - so extremely uptight she opposes music as a concept

jokes.text: If these walls could talk, they would say, 'This is a magical place. You are safe here. We are talking walls. We're not going to eat you.'
jokes.setup: Michael's inspirational speech about his disco
jokes.punchline: Gets sidetracked imagining walls reassuring people they won't eat them
jokes.explanation: Fantastic Michael logic - reassuring walls become creepy by addressing cannibalism

jokes.text: This is oil from the gland of an otter. It keeps their fur water-resistant as well as traps heat.
jokes.setup: Dwight applying treatment to Phyllis
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions using otter gland oil on human
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight alternative medicine with disturbing animal details

jokes.text: Boss, this used to hang from my windshield, but it belongs in here. Hey, thank you, Creed. You really get this place. No problem. I'll just have no idea who's driving behind me now.
jokes.setup: Creed donating something to the disco
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was his rear-view mirror, causing safety issue
jokes.explanation: Perfect Creed moment - generous gesture creates dangerous driving situation

jokes.text: I find the rattle soothing. Puts me to sleep.
jokes.setup: Discussing snake choices while Phyllis recovers
jokes.punchline: She finds rattlesnake sounds relaxing
jokes.explanation: Good Phyllis character moment - unexpectedly finds danger soothing

jokes.text: I think Bob's gonna cheat on me with his new secretary. What's so funny? When I say it out loud, it's so silly.
jokes.setup: Phyllis sharing serious marital concern
jokes.punchline: Realizes her fear sounds silly when spoken aloud
jokes.explanation: Nice moment of self-awareness from Phyllis, though not particularly funny

jokes.text: You are forcing me to be down here. Am I not allowed to have some fun? No cleaning up.
jokes.setup: Angela trying to clean the disco
jokes.punchline: Michael won't let her clean, which is her idea of fun
jokes.explanation: Perfect Angela moment - cleaning is literally her version of fun at a party

jokes.text: You can't steal what is legally your property.
jokes.setup: Jim asking to 'steal' his wife Pam for a dance
jokes.punchline: Dwight's literal legal interpretation of marriage
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight literalism turning romantic phrase into property law

jokes.text: What dance competition? I was just dancing casually with my friends, ya!
jokes.setup: Dwight mentions dance competition
jokes.punchline: Jim's denial while clearly competitive dancing
jokes.explanation: Jim being competitive while denying it, solid character moment

jokes.text: Are you sure that's not the gay ear? Are you 12 years old?
jokes.setup: Andy getting his ear pierced
jokes.punchline: Worried about old stereotype, Dwight's dismissive response
jokes.explanation: Dated joke about ear piercing stereotypes, not particularly clever

jokes.text: Ow, son of a bitch! Andy, that was just the ice.
jokes.setup: Andy preparing for ear piercing
jokes.punchline: He screams in pain from the numbing ice, not the actual piercing
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup showing Andy's low pain tolerance
